title: Velvety Tunisian Couscous Pilaf
description: A roasted, pillowy couscous pilaf infused with allspice berries, kombu cold extract, and sweet dried apricots, perfect for a comforting, Jain-friendly dinner.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine the crushed allspice berries with the kombu cold extract and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a dry skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the couscous to the skillet and stir to coat, then toast for 2-3 minutes until lightly golden.
Pour in the water, stir, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes until the water is absorbed.
Fluff the cooked couscous with a fork, then fold in the allspice-kombu mixture, lemon zest, and lemon juice.
Stir in the chopped dried apricots and toasted pistachios, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Gently fold in the fresh parsley for a bright finish.
Divide the pilaf among plates or a serving dish and garnish with additional pistachios if desired.
Serve hot as a comforting, pillowy side or main dish.
